description: HTTP webhook delivery for order and driver lifecycle events, allowing external systems to react to ride state changes in real time.

actors.name: Operator
actors.description: Configures webhook endpoints for their organization.

actors.name: Platform
actors.description: Delivers signed webhook payloads to registered endpoints on event occurrence.

actors.name: Receiver
actors.description: External service or application that receives and processes webhooks.

fields.name: endpoint_url
fields.label: HTTPS URL that will receive webhook POST requests.

fields.name: events
fields.label: List of event types this endpoint subscribes to. Empty means all events.

fields.name: mode
fields.label: Whether this endpoint is live or in test mode.

fields.name: version
fields.label: API version for the webhook payload format.

fields.name: status
fields.label: Whether this endpoint is active or disabled.

fields.name: api_credential_id
fields.label: The API credential this endpoint is scoped to.

rules.rule_01: Webhook endpoints are scoped to an organization; each organization manages its own endpoints.
rules.rule_02: An endpoint may subscribe to a specific list of event types or receive all events (empty events array).
rules.rule_03: The platform delivers events as HTTP POST requests to the registered URL with a JSON payload.
rules.rule_04: Delivery includes the full resource payload for the affected entity (order or driver).
rules.rule_05: Failed deliveries are logged in the webhook request log with status code and response body.
rules.rule_06: Event types are fixed and defined at the platform level; custom event types are not supported.
rules.rule_07: Endpoints can be filtered by mode (live vs test) to separate production and test traffic.

given: operator provides a valid HTTPS URL

then.description: Endpoint is stored with URL, event subscriptions, mode, and status.

endpoint_registered.result: Endpoint is active and will receive matching events from this point forward.

given: a lifecycle event fires (e.g., order.dispatched, order.completed)
one or more active endpoints subscribe to this event type

then.description: An HTTP POST request is sent to each subscribed endpoint URL with the event payload.
Delivery attempt is logged with timestamp, response code, and response body.

event_delivered.result: External system receives the event payload and can react (e.g., update its own database, trigger actions).

given: the endpoint URL returns a non-2xx status or times out

then.description: Failed attempt is logged with error details.

delivery_failed.result: Operator can inspect the log and debug the endpoint configuration.

given: endpoint is registered with an empty events list
all_events_subscribed.result: Endpoint receives every event type fired by the platform.

errors.message: The provided webhook URL is not a valid HTTPS URL.
The specified webhook endpoint could not be found.

events.name: order.dispatched
events.description: An order has been dispatched to a driver.

events.name: order.driver_assigned
events.description: A driver has been assigned to an order.

events.name: order.updated
events.description: An order's status or data has changed.

events.name: order.dispatch_failed
events.description: Order dispatch failed to find a driver.

events.name: driver.assigned
events.description: A driver has been assigned to an entity or order.

related.reason: Lifecycle events are the source of webhook delivery.
Webhook endpoints are scoped per organization.
API credentials scope which endpoints can register webhooks.

goals.description: HTTP webhook delivery for order and driver lifecycle events, allowing external systems to react to ride state changes in real time.

success_metrics.measurement: Successful operations divided by total attempts
Errors that auto-recover without manual intervention

constraints.description: Must degrade gracefully when dependencies are unavailable

tradeoffs.reason: integration failures can cascade across systems
